title: Account validity should be verified when authenticating users with PAM
description: Account validity should be verified when authenticating users with PAMAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalsecurityVulnerabilitycwesymbolic-executionPluggable authentication module (PAM) is a mechanism used on many UNIX variants to provide a unified way to authenticate users, independently of the underlying authentication scheme.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen authenticating users, if the validity of the account is not checked (not locked, not expired …​), it may lead to unauthorized access to resources.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cook
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: PAM authentication detected. Verify that pam_acct_mgmt() is called after successful pam_authenticate() to check account validity (expired, locked, etc.).
